text: The RMS value of an AC voltage \( V = V_0\sin(\omega t) \) is:
explanation: RMS value: \( V_{rms} = V_0/\sqrt{2} \). For Indian household supply: \( V_{rms}=220\,\text{V} \Rightarrow V_0\approx 311\,\text{V} \).
name: RMS Voltage

text: The impedance of a series LCR circuit is:
explanation: LCR impedance: \( Z = \sqrt{R^2+(X_L-X_C)^2} \), where \( X_L=\omega L \) and \( X_C=1/\omega C \).
name: LCR Impedance

text: At resonance in an LCR series circuit:
options: \( X_L = X_C \Rightarrow Z = R \) (minimum impedance, maximum current)
explanation: Resonance: \( X_L = X_C \Rightarrow \omega_0 = 1/\sqrt{LC} \). Impedance = \(R\) (minimum). Current = \(V/R\) (maximum). Power factor = 1.
name: LCR Resonance

text: The resonant frequency of an LCR circuit is:
explanation: Resonant frequency: \( f_0 = \frac{1}{2\pi\sqrt{LC}} \). \( \omega_0 = 1/\sqrt{LC} \). Used in radio tuning circuits.
name: Resonant Frequency

explanation: Inductive reactance: \( X_L = \omega L \). Increases with frequency. Unit: Ohm. Lags current behind voltage by 90°.
name: Inductive Reactance

explanation: Capacitive reactance: \( X_C = 1/\omega C \). Decreases with frequency. Leads voltage — current leads voltage by 90°.
name: Capacitive Reactance

text: Power factor of an AC circuit is defined as:
explanation: Power factor: \( \cos\phi = R/Z \). Average power: \( P = V_{rms}I_{rms}\cos\phi \). For pure R: \(\cos\phi=1\). For pure L or C: \(\cos\phi=0\).
name: Power Factor

text: The average power dissipated in an AC circuit is:
explanation: Average power: \( P = V_{rms}I_{rms}\cos\phi = I_{rms}^2 R \). Also called true power (watts). Apparent power = \(V_{rms}I_{rms}\) (volt-ampere, VA).
name: Average Power in AC

text: The Q-factor (quality factor) of an LCR circuit at resonance is:
explanation: Q-factor: \( Q = \omega_0 L/R = \frac{1}{R}\sqrt{L/C} \). Higher Q → sharper resonance peak → better selectivity (important in radio tuning).
name: Q-Factor

text: The phase difference between voltage and current in a pure inductor is:
explanation: Pure inductor: \( V = L\frac{dI}{dt} \). If \( I = I_0\sin\omega t \), then \( V = LI_0\omega\cos\omega t \). Voltage leads current by 90°.
name: Phase in Pure Inductor

text: The wattless current (reactive current) in an AC circuit flows through:
options: Inductance and capacitance (pure L or pure C)
explanation: Wattless (reactive) current: component that does no real work (90° out of phase with voltage). Flows through L and C. Power factor = 0.
name: Wattless Current

text: The transformer equation \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 \) comes from:
options: Faraday's law applied to both primary and secondary
explanation: Ideal transformer: same flux links both coils. \( \varepsilon_1 = N_1 d\Phi/dt \), \( \varepsilon_2 = N_2 d\Phi/dt \). Dividing: \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 \).
name: Transformer Equation Derivation

text: An LC circuit oscillates with time period \( T = \)
explanation: LC oscillation: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{LC} \), \( f = 1/(2\pi\sqrt{LC}) \). Energy oscillates between electric (capacitor) and magnetic (inductor).
name: LC Oscillation Period

text: The form factor of a sinusoidal AC wave is:
explanation: Form factor \( = V_{rms}/V_{avg} = \frac{V_0/\sqrt{2}}{2V_0/\pi} = \frac{\pi}{2\sqrt{2}} \approx 1.11 \). Peak factor \( = V_0/V_{rms} = \sqrt{2} \approx 1.414 \).
name: Form Factor

text: Choke coil (used to control AC without power loss) has:
options: Low resistance, high inductance (high reactance, low power factor)
explanation: Choke coil: high \(L\), low \(R\). High \(X_L = \omega L\) limits AC current with minimal heat loss. Power factor ≈ 0, very efficient AC current controller.
name: Choke Coil
